data.title: Meta set to overtake Google in digital ad revenues for the first time
data.description:
Meta (META) is on track to overtake Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) in terms of total digital advertising revenues by the end of the year, according to the latest forecast by market research firm Emarketer.

The Facebook owner is forecast to reach $243.46B in net worldwide ad revenues in 2026, up from $196.17B in 2025.

Google's (GOOGL) net global ad revenues are expected to hit $239.54B in 2026, compared to $214.06B in 2025.

Meta (META) is also set to become the top digital advertising engine, with its share in global ad spend seen reaching 26.8% in 2026. Google's (GOOGL) share is forecast to hit 26.4%.

Zach Goldner, senior forecasting analyst at Emarketer, noted that Meta (META) is unlocking more value across its entire ecosystem at the same time.

"Tools like its Advantage+, AI-generated ad creatives, and its broader automation stack are improving performance across both Facebook and Instagram, with Reels being a big beneficiary," Goldner said. "As a result, advertisers are getting better bang for their buck, and that's pulling more ad dollars onto the platform."

"Google has plenty of levers it can pull to try to speed up growth," said Max Willens, principal analyst, Emarketer. "But the diversity of its business — it generates billions of dollars in subscriber revenues from YouTube Premium, for example — may make it harder for it to leapfrog past Meta in terms of digital ad revenues."

Meanwhile, Emarketer forecast Amazon (AMZN) earning $82.07B in worldwide ad revenues this year, up from $68.64B last year. Its share of global digital ad spending is expected to hit 9% in 2026.

Meta (META), Google (GOOGL) and Amazon (AMZN) together will represent 62.3% of worldwide digital ad spending in 2026.

"The consolidation of digital ad dollars around Google, Meta, and Amazon reflects a compounding advantage of first-party data, AI integrations, and audience reach," said Drew Spink, senior forecasting analyst, Emarketer.

data.title: Stock index futures advance on hopes of U.S.-Iran peace talks
data.description:
Stock index futures gained on Tuesday as investors grew optimistic about potential U.S.-Iran peace talks that could ease tensions in the Middle East.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) rose +0.41%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) advanced +0.25%, and Dow futures (INDU) edged up +0.20%.

The gains followed comments from President Donald Trump indicating that Iran had reached out for potential negotiations, even as the U.S. moved forward with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Asian equities also rallied on hopes that a deal could lower oil prices and support global growth.

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower across the curve. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) slipped to 4.28%, down 1.3 basis points. The 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell to 3.77%, also down 1.3 basis points, while the 30-year Treasury yield (US30Y) dipped to 4.88%, declining 1.6 basis points.

Markets are also awaiting the Producer Price Index report, with expectations for a 1.2% monthly rise in PPI-FD driven by surging energy costs. Core PPI, excluding food and energy, is forecast at +0.5% for the month and +4.2% year-over-year.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Sandisk (SNDK) +3.31%, Oracle (ORCL) +2.09%, and Brown & Brown (BRO) +2.09%. Decliners included NXP Semiconductors (NXPI) -3.85%, Incyte (INCY) -2.62%, and Equifax (EFX) -2.25%.

data.title: Eurofins Scientific divests electrical testing business in €575M deal with UL Solutions
Bitcoin rises to four-week high on hopes of US-Iran peace talks
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC-USD) rose to a four-week high as risk assets rallied on hopes the U.S. could reach a deal with Iran to ease tensions.

The largest cryptocurrency climbed to $74,929, its highest level since March 17, before trimming gains to trade around $74,726 at press time on Tuesday. Smaller tokens also moved higher, with Ethereum (ETH) rising to $2,381.

The advance followed comments from U.S. President Donald Trump that Iran had reached out for potential peace talks, even as the U.S. moved ahead with a naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Asian equities also rose on optimism that a deal could ease oil prices and support global growth.

Despite a sharp drop from its October peak of $126K, Bitcoin (BTC-USD) has traded in a relatively tight range over the past two months. Still, it has outperformed many traditional assets since the U.S.-Iran conflict escalated in late February. The token is up more than 10% since February 27, while gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has fallen nearly 10% and the S&P 500 (SP500) has remained roughly flat over the same period.

data.title: South Korea's AI chip startup DeepX readies IPO, explores U.S. market entry
data.description:
• South Korean AI chip startup DeepX is preparing to go public via a domestic initial public offering and said it is open to a possible U.S. listing later, underscoring ambitions to tap deeper capital pools and expand its global footprint.
  • The company, which develops on-device AI chips designed for edge computing applications, said it will appoint underwriters after wrapping up its ongoing funding round in H1 2026. 
  • DeepX claims its chips significantly reduce energy consumption and cost compared with traditional GPUs, positioning it as a challenger in a market dominated by large companies.
  • The startup works with companies like Hyundai and Baidu (BIDU), DeepX founder and CEO Lokwon Kim told Reuters.
  • A potential U.S. listing, while not yet committed, would align the South Korean company with a broader trend among Asian semiconductor firms seeking higher valuations and capital.

data.title: Québec Innovative Materials announces C$15M bought deal private placement
Spain inflation rises to 3.4% in March
BP sees “exceptional” trading in Q1; net debt rises to $25B - $27B
data.description:
BP (BP) Tuesday said that its oil trading division is set for an “exceptional” first-quarter performance, with earnings boosted by volatility in energy markets amid Middle East tensions.

Rising geopolitical conflict in the region has jolted energy markets, with Brent and crude oil prices seeing significant increases. Brent (CO1:COM) averaged $81.13/bbl in the first quarter compared to $63.73/bbl in the fourth quarter. The BP RIM averaged $16.9/bbl in Q1 '26 vs. $15.2/bbl in Q4 '25, the company said.

The oil major's reported upstream production in the first quarter is expected to be broadly flat compared to the fourth quarter 2025 (2,344 mboe/d). Within this, gas & low-carbon energy is expected to be slightly higher compared to the fourth quarter of 2025 (788 mboe/d), and oil production & operations is expected to be slightly lower compared to the fourth quarter of 2025 (1,555 mboe/d), including price impacts on PSA and TSC entitlement volumes.

In the gas & low carbon energy segment, realizations are expected to be broadly flat compared to the prior quarter. However, oil production & operations segment realizations are expected to have an impact compared to the prior quarter in the range of +$0.1B to $0.2B.

"These include the significant impact of the price lagsa on BP’s production, particularly in the Gulf of America and the UAE where production is priced on one and two month lagged basis respectively. Compared to the prior quarter, reflecting production mix, cash costs are $0.1B higher and DD&A charge is broadly flat (fourth quarter 2025 $2B) due to a higher unit charge."

Net debt at the end of Q1 is expected to rise to $25B - $27B from $22.2B at the end of Q4 2025, driven mainly by a $4B - $7B increase in working capital due to the prevailing price environment.

data.title: Texas AG investigating if Lululemon's activewear contains 'forever chemicals'
data.description:
Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is investigating whether Lululemon Athletica's (LULU) athletic apparel contains "forever chemicals," which its customers would not expect based on the brand's marketing, a claim that the company rejected.

The Office of the Attorney General said emerging research and consumer concerns raised questions about the potential presence of these chemicals, called PFAS, in Lululemon's (LULU) apparel.

PFAS do not break down easily in the environment and have been linked to endocrine disruption, infertility, cancer, and other health issues.

The AG's investigation will also check Lululemon's (LULU) Restricted Substances List, testing protocols and supply chain practices to determine if its products comply with its stated safety standards.

"I will not allow any corporation to sell harmful, toxic materials to consumers at a premium price under the guise of wellness and sustainability," said Paxton. "If Lululemon has violated Texas law, it will be held accountable."

Lululemon (LULU) said it does not use PFAS in its products, adding that it "phased out the substance in FY23, which had been used in durable water repellent products."

data.title: Novo Nordisk teams up with OpenAI to boost AI-driven drug discovery efforts
data.description:
Danish drugmaker Novo Nordisk (NVO) on Tuesday announced a strategic partnership with OpenAI (OPENAI) aimed at accelerating its AI transformation in healthcare and speeding up the development of new treatment options for patients.

The partnership will apply advanced AI capabilities to analyse complex datasets, identify promising drug candidates, and reduce the time required to move from research to patient. It has been structured with strict data protection, governance, and human oversight to ensure ethical and compliant use, the companies said.

“Integrating AI in our everyday work gives us the ability to analyse datasets at a scale that was previously impossible, identify patterns we could not see, and test hypotheses faster than ever,” said Novo Nordisk (NVO) CEO Mike Doustdar.

OpenAI will assist Novo Nordisk in upskilling the company’s global workforce and enhancing AI literacy. The partnership will also apply OpenAI’s capabilities to improve efficiency in manufacturing, supply chain and distribution, and corporate operations.

Pilot programmes will launch across research & development, manufacturing, and commercial operations, with full integration by the end of 2026.

“AI is reshaping industries and in life sciences, it can help people live better, longer lives,” said Sam Altman, CEO of OpenAI (OPENAI). “This collaboration with Novo Nordisk will help them accelerate scientific discovery, run smarter global operations, and redefine the future of patient care.”
